title: Genesis: Flood and New Beginning
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of genesis: flood and new beginning and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study genesis: flood and new beginning and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: What sign did God give Noah as a guarantee that he would never again destroy the earth by flood? Answer: a rainbow.
acceptable_variants: god set his bow in the cloud

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: God's covenant with Noah and all living creatures after the flood, promising never to destroy the earth by flood again; the rainbow is its sign
The beings described in Genesis 6:4 whose presence marks the corruption that preceded the flood; their exact identity is debated among scholars
